component.name: Skerry
component.summary: Cross-platform SSH client with a single core

description.p: Skerry is an open-source SSH client built on a single Kotlin Multiplatform core with a Compose Multiplatform UI, sharing one codebase across Desktop and Android.

ul.li: SSH (sshj + BouncyCastle) and SSH certificates
SFTP with a dual-pane commander
Port forwarding: local (-L), remote (-R), and dynamic/SOCKS (-D)
Telnet and serial connections
Custom terminal emulation: VT line-drawing, Unicode, SGR, OSC 8/4/52, bracketed paste
Session tabs with split view, SSH auto-reconnect, and live host metrics
Zero-knowledge vault (XChaCha20-Poly1305): keys, passwords, identities, certificates
Optional self-hosted, zero-knowledge sync
Optional AI assistant with per-host policies, including a fully local on-device model

screenshot.caption: Terminal with host manager, session tabs, and live metrics

ul.li: VNC remote desktop: built-in RFB client with clipboard, quality presets and resize-to-window
Themes: light theme (Daybreak), theme catalog, live System mode
Session recording and playback (asciinema v2), broadcast mode, command palette
Snippet categories and run-time variables; host monitor and service discovery
SFTP file viewer and editor; rename vault secrets; change account password
Simplified Chinese localization and a new UI font (IBM Plex Sans)
Sync hardening: single account/vault password, token refresh, self-healing retries
Local AI moved into an isolated process — no more native crashes during inference
Vault records fully authenticated: the AEAD tag now binds all record metadata
Host-key checks fail closed while the vault is locked
OSC 52 clipboard writes are opt-in; copied passwords clear after 30 seconds
Team key rotates on member removal; sync transport hardened
Android terminal settings on par with desktop (scrollback depth, cursor style)
Mosh support: native client, sessions survive network drops and roaming
SSH jump hosts (ProxyJump) with host-key verification per hop
Per-host keep-alive with auto-reconnect; passive update notifications
Custom window chrome on desktop; clickable URLs in the terminal
Linux AppImage and Flatpak packaging; fixed Windows MSI build
Type-to-jump path bar in SFTP and Files; smoother terminal rendering
Weak master passwords blocked at vault creation; modal dismissal fixes
